No Poverty.reason: The course syllabus contains no explicit or implicit content related to poverty eradication, social protection, or economic resources for the poor.

Zero Hunger.reason: There is no mention of food security, nutrition, sustainable agriculture, or food waste in the course description or schedule.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course content does not address health, well-being, disease prevention, or access to healthcare services.

Quality Education.reason: The course is a university-level elective (tertiary education, Target 4.3) focused on developing professional communication, problem-solving, and writing skills, which are relevant for employment and entrepreneurship (Target 4.4).
evidence: Business Writing and Oral Training II
Students will acquire professional communication skills.
Students will acquire problem solving skills.

Gender Equality.reason: The syllabus does not contain any content or objectives related to gender equality, women's empowerment, or addressing discrimination.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: There is no mention of water resources, sanitation, hygiene, or pollution in the course material.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: The course syllabus does not include any topics related to energy access, renewable energy, or energy efficiency.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course explicitly covers entrepreneurship, including 'Starting Your Own Business' and 'Turning Problems Into Business Ideas,' directly supporting Target 8.3 on promoting productive activities, decent job creation, and entrepreneurship.

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course promotes innovation through topics like 'The World of AI' and 'Be An Entrepreneur,' which encourages new ideas and business development, aligning with Target 9.5 (enhance scientific research, upgrade technological capabilities, encouraging innovation) and indirectly Target 9.3 (access of small-scale enterprises to financial services and integration into value chains).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course content does not address income inequality, social inclusion, or discriminatory practices.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: There is no content related to urban planning, housing, transport, heritage, or disaster resilience in the syllabus.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: Core Competence B explicitly states students will 'understand and be able to identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues,' directly contributing to awareness for sustainable development (Target 12.8).
evidence: 理解與辨別道德、社會責任與永續議題(Students will understand and be able to identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues.)

Climate Action.reason: While 'sustainability issues' are mentioned (SDG 12), there is no specific content on climate change mitigation, adaptation, or awareness-raising related to climate action.

Life Below Water.reason: The course syllabus does not contain any topics related to marine ecosystems, ocean health, or sustainable use of marine resources.

Life on Land.reason: There is no content related to terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, deforestation, or land degradation in the course material.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course does not address violence, justice, rule of law, corruption, or institutional development.

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course explicitly fosters 'team spirit' and 'teamwork' through group activities, which, while at a micro-level, aligns with the foundational principles of collaboration and partnership outlined in Target 17.17.
evidence: work... in groups to develop team spirit
E. 團隊合作與領導能力(Students will be able to work effectively in teams as well as develop leadership skills.)
